title: Cajanus albicans
intro: Cajanus albicans, also known as the white pigeon pea or white cajan, is a type of legume plant native to tropical Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including treating fever, inflammation, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of C. albicans include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, C. albicans is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in decoctions and poultices to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Cajanus albicans is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Cajanus, which contains about 30 species of plants native to tropical Asia. Cajanus albicans is further classified as a member of the tribe Phaseoleae and the subfamily Faboideae. The plant is native to Australia and Southeast Asia, and it is characterized by its white or yellowish flowers. Within the genus Cajanus, it is placed as a distinct species due to its unique characteristics and distribution.
